annotations.label: SOF – Marriage Provision (Unenforceable, correct)
annotations.rationale: Correctly applies SoF marriage provision and notes lack of sufficient detrimental reliance to invoke PE exception.

annotations.label: SOF – Part Performance Exception (Enforceable)
annotations.rationale: Concludes enforceable because full performance takes contract out of SoF. Wrong: part-performance doctrine does not apply to the marriage provision of SoF; oral promise remains unenforceable.

annotations.label: Main Purpose Doctrine – tax motivation (Enforceable)
annotations.rationale: Argues tax motive triggers main-purpose doctrine. Wrong doctrine applied to wrong provision.

annotations.label: SOF – Assumption of Debt (Unenforceable, wrong rule)
annotations.rationale: Correct outcome but wrong doctrinal hook (assumption of another's debt rather than consideration of marriage).

annotations.label: Promissory Estoppel (Enforceable)
annotations.rationale: Concludes enforceable purely on promissory estoppel without adequately addressing SoF bar.

annotations.label: Promissory Estoppel (Enforceable)
annotations.rationale: Holds enforceable via promissory estoppel on reliance. Wrong: PE does not override SoF marriage provision in most jurisdictions.

annotations.label: Main Purpose / Leading-Object Exception (Enforceable)
annotations.rationale: Invokes leading-object/main-purpose exception. Wrong: that exception applies to suretyship, not marriage provision.

annotations.label: SOF – Marriage Provision (Unenforceable)
annotations.rationale: Correctly identifies SoF makes oral promise unenforceable; top topic 'Suretyship' reveals the specific provision cited is often wrong (suretyship rather than marriage), but conclusion is correct.

annotations.label: SOF – Marriage Provision, explicitly correct
annotations.rationale: Correctly applies SoF marriage provision: oral promise made in consideration of marriage must be in writing.

annotations.label: Original Promise Doctrine (Enforceable)
annotations.rationale: Invokes original-promise (as opposed to collateral-promise/suretyship) to find no SoF bar. Wrong provision analyzed.

annotations.label: Past Consideration (Unenforceable, wrong reason)
annotations.rationale: Arrives at unenforceable but for wrong reason (past consideration). The son had not yet performed when the promise was made, so consideration was present; SoF is the operative bar.

annotations.label: Valid Oral Contract / Substantial Performance (Enforceable)
annotations.rationale: Ignores SoF entirely; concludes valid oral contract enforceable on general contract elements plus substantial performance.

annotations.label: Consideration Present – Oral Enforceable
annotations.rationale: Finds consideration sufficient but fails to apply SoF marriage provision as dispositive bar.

annotations.label: SOF – Suretyship Provision (Unenforceable, wrong rule)
annotations.rationale: Reaches correct 'unenforceable' conclusion but frames issue as suretyship (promise to pay debt of another) rather than marriage provision. Wrong SoF category.

annotations.label: Promissory Estoppel (Enforceable)
annotations.rationale: Promissory estoppel cited to overcome SoF. Most jurisdictions do not allow PE to circumvent SoF marriage provision.
